heading: Same Truth Conditions
body: **Logical equivalence** means that two formulas have the same truth value in every row of a truth table. If one is true exactly when the other is true, they are logically equivalent.

examples.text: If two final columns are both `T F F T`, the formulas are logically equivalent.
If one final column is `T F F T` and the other is `T T F T`, the formulas are logically equivalent.

heading: Compare Final Columns
body: To test equivalence, compare the final main-operator columns row by row. If every row matches, the formulas are equivalent. If even one row differs, they are not equivalent.

examples.text: `T F T F` and `T F T F` match in every row.
`T F T F` and `T F F F` match in every row.

note: Only the final columns decide the result.

heading: A Standard Pair
body: Some different formulas produce the same final column. That is enough for equivalence.

examples.text: $\sim (P \vee Q)$ and $(\sim P \wedge \sim Q)$ are equivalent if their final columns match row for row.
Different symbol shapes always mean different truth conditions.

note: Equivalent formulas can look very different.

heading: One Mismatch Fails
body: A common mistake is calling two formulas equivalent because most rows match. That is not enough. Equivalence requires a perfect row-by-row match in the final columns.

examples.text: `T T F T` and `T T F T` are equivalent.
`T T F T` and `T F F T` are equivalent because only one row differs.

heading: Meaning Over Appearance
body: **Truth conditions** are the circumstances under which a formula is true or false. Logical equivalence depends on truth conditions, not on word order, symbol count, or visual similarity.

examples.text: Two formulas may be equivalent even if one uses negation and the other does not.
Two formulas are equivalent only when they are written in exactly the same way.

heading: Core Test
body: Build the truth tables, then compare the final columns row by row. If every row matches, the formulas are logically equivalent. If any row differs, they are not.
